title: Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Data Center Moratorium Act appears to be a policy that temporarily halts or restricts the building of new artificial intelligence data centers.

title: Workforce for AI Trust Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: This document likely deals with the creation of a standardized workforce for the development and implementation of artificial intelligence systems in accordance with trust and security guidelines. The Act would aim to establish a reliable and trustworthy workforce for creating responsible AI systems.

title: READ AI Models Act
action: Ordered to be Reported in the Nature of a Substitute (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 35 - 0.
summary: The READ AI Models Act is a policy aimed at overseeing and managing artificial intelligence models in a specific context. The details of the policies and regulations under the READ AI Models Act are not provided.

title: To direct the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to seek to enter into an agreement with a federally funded research and development center for the conduct of an independent evaluation of artificial intelligence systems in use by the Veterans Health Administration, and for other purposes.
action: Referred to the House Committee on Veterans' Affairs.
summary: This bill directs the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to find a non-profit research center to evaluate the artificial intelligence systems used by the Veterans Health Administration. The goal is to ensure the systems are functioning as intended.

title: A bill to support an interdisciplinary research community for the advancement of artificial intelligence and AI-powered innovation through partnerships and capacity building at certain institutions of higher education and other institutions, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: This bill aims to support research and innovation in artificial intelligence by partnering with institutions of higher education and other organizations. It aims to advance AI capabilities and create capacity-building programs at participating institutions.

title: A bill to exempt AI data centers from bonus depreciation and require data center operators to submit certain information relating to electricity and water use by data centers, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill aims to change tax rules for AI data centers by removing tax breaks and requiring operators to report their energy use. It also covers other unrelated purposes, but the specific details are not provided.

title: A bill to prohibit the use of certain artificial intelligence models across the Federal Government, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Homeland Security and Governmental Affairs.
summary: This bill aims to prohibit the use of certain artificial intelligence models across the Federal Government, with the intention of regulating their use.

title: A bill to establish a National Workforce Transition Board to support training and education activities for workers, in response to the adoption of artificial intelligence and emerging technology, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance.
summary: This bill aims to create a National Workforce Transition Board to help workers adapt to jobs affected by artificial intelligence and emerging technologies. The board would support training and education activities for workers in response to these changes.

title: A bill to require the Director of the National Institute of Standards and Technology to establish a working group to facilitate coordination among relevant stakeholders regarding compound risks relating to artificial intelligence physical systems, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: This bill aims to establish a working group at the National Institute of Standards and Technology to coordinate efforts on compound risks associated with artificial intelligence and physical systems. The working group will facilitate collaboration among relevant stakeholders for other purposes.

title: A bill to improve the tracking and processing of security and safety incidents and risks associated with artificial intelligence, and for other purposes.
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation.
summary: This bill aims to improve the tracking and processing of security and safety incidents related to artificial intelligence. It is intended to enhance the handling of AI-related risks and incidents for other unspecified purposes.

title: Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Civil Rights Act of 2025 aims to regulate the development and use of artificial intelligence to protect human rights and prevent discrimination. This legislation is focused on ensuring accountability and fairness in AI-driven decision-making systems.

title: Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on Science, Space, and Technology, and in addition to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Artificial Intelligence Environmental Impacts Act of 2026 aims to address the potential environmental consequences of artificial intelligence development and use. It is likely to establish regulations and guidelines to mitigate the environmental effects of AI technologies.

title: Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026
action: Referred to the Committee on the Judiciary, and in addition to the Committee on Oversight and Government Reform, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Sectoral AI Governance Act of 2026 appears to be a law that aims to establish guidelines for Artificial Intelligence (AI) usage within specific industries. However, without more information, the exact scope and provisions of the Act remain unknown.

title: K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 18 - 15.
summary: The K–12 AI Literacy and Readiness Act of 2026 is a proposed law aiming to improve education in artificial intelligence (AI) and prepare students for its impact in society. This law likely seeks to incorporate AI education into US K-12 school curricula and help students develop essential AI literacy skills.

title: SBA AI Adoption Reporting Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Small Business.
summary: The SBA AI Adoption Reporting Act of 2026 is a bill that requires businesses to report their use and adoption of artificial intelligence. The purpose of this act is to increase transparency and understanding of AI's role in the economy.

title: Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act
action: Forwarded by Subcommittee to Full Committee (Amended) by Voice Vote.
summary: The Protecting Families from AI Data Center Energy Costs Act aims to reduce energy costs associated with AI data centers to protect families financially. This act likely seeks to make data centers more energy-efficient, potentially lowering costs for households.

title: Conversational AI Services Act
action: Referred to the House Committee on Energy and Commerce.
summary: The Conversational AI Services Act is a law that aims to regulate and govern the use of conversational artificial intelligence services. It likely focuses on setting standards, ensuring user safety, and establishing guidelines for companies offering conversational AI services.

title: Homestake AI Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources.
summary: The Homestake AI Act of 2026 is a policy document that likely addresses regulations and guidelines for artificial intelligence. Its exact content is unknown due to the lack of available description.

title: Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026
action: Read twice and referred to the Committee on Armed Services.
summary: The Responsible Artificial Intelligence Defense Act of 2026 is a policy aimed at regulating the use of artificial intelligence in a responsible manner. The exact scope and details of the policy are not provided.

title: SBA Artificial Intelligence Utilization Act of 2026
action: Received in the Senate and Read twice and referred to the Committee on Small Business and Entrepreneurship.
summary: This document is related to a federal law, specifically the policy on using AI in the Small Business Administration (SBA), and its purpose is to utilize artificial intelligence within the SBA.

title: FARM AI Act of 2026
action: Referred to the House Committee on Agriculture.
summary: The FARM AI Act of 2026 aims to regulate the development and deployment of Artificial Intelligence in the agricultural industry. The act likely establishes guidelines and standards to ensure AI systems are used safely, securely, and efficiently in farming and agricultural practices.

title: American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act
action: Referred to the Subcommittee on Oversight and Investigations.
summary: The American Artificial Intelligence Leadership and Uniformity Act aims to establish guidelines for the development and use of artificial intelligence in the US, promoting leadership and consistency in the field. The specific details and scope of the act are unclear without more information, but its intention is to regulate and advance AI in a unified manner.

title: Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act of 2025
action: Referred to the Committee on Energy and Commerce, and in addition to the Committees on the Judiciary, and Education and Workforce, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provisions as fall within the jurisdiction of the committee concerned.
summary: The Stop AI Price Gouging and Wage Fixing Act of 2025 aims to prevent abusive business practices involving artificial intelligence, such as price gouging and wage fixing. The goal is to protect consumers and workers from being exploited by AI-powered companies.

title: Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026
action: Ordered to be Reported (Amended) by the Yeas and Nays: 43 - 0.
summary: The Deterring American AI Model Theft Act of 2026 is a policy aiming to prevent the theft of American Artificial Intelligence models. This act likely focuses on protecting US-developed AI technology from unauthorized use and exploitation.
